chunks.text: Michael A. Lieberman is Professor Emeritus in the Department of Electrical Engineering and Computer Sciences at UC Berkeley. His contact information includes office location at 261M Cory Hall, telephone 510-643-6632, email lieber@eecs.berkeley.edu, and office hours on Thursdays 3-4pm. Research support contact is Annie Ren at 353 Cory Hall with email annie@erso.berkeley.edu.
chunks.question: What is Michael Lieberman's position and contact details?
chunks.answer: Professor Emeritus at UC Berkeley EECS.

chunks.text: Michael Lieberman earned his B.S. and M.S. from MIT in 1962 and his Ph.D. from MIT in 1966. He joined UC Berkeley's EECS department in 1966. In 1971, he received UC Berkeley's Distinguished Teaching Award. He was a Guggenheim Fellow during 1972-73. He is a Fellow of multiple professional societies including APS, AAAS, IEEE, American Vacuum Society, International Plasma Chemistry Society, and Institute of Physics (Great Britain).
chunks.question: What are Michael Lieberman's educational background and early career?
chunks.answer: MIT degrees, joined UC Berkeley in 1966.

chunks.text: Lieberman received the IEEE Plasma Science and Applications Award in 1995, the von Engel Prize of the International Union of Pure and Applied Physics in 2005, and the Will Allis Prize for Study of Ionized Gases in 2006. He collaborated with Prof. A.J. Lichtenberg on nonlinear dynamics research. Their co-authored monographs include 'Regular and Stochastic Motion' (1983), 'Regular and Chaotic Dynamics' (1991), and 'Principles of Plasma Discharges and Materials Processing' (1994 with expanded editions).
chunks.question: What major awards did Lieberman receive and with whom did he collaborate?
chunks.answer: IEEE, von Engel, Will Allis prizes; collaborated with Lichtenberg.

chunks.text: Lieberman's research area is Power and Energy (ENE) with focus on plasma-assisted materials processing. Selected publications include multiple editions of 'Principles of Plasma Discharges and Materials Processing' (1994, 2005) and 'Regular and Chaotic Dynamics' (1992) co-authored with A.J. Lichtenberg. These works were published by Wiley and Springer-Verlag.
chunks.question: What is Lieberman's research focus and key publications?

chunks.text: Selected honors and awards include: AVS Plasma Science and Technology Division Plasma Prize (2022), NPSS Marie Sklodowska-Curie Award (2020), Will Allis Prize (2006), Von Engel Prize (2005), American Vacuum Society Fellow (2000), NPSS Plasma Science & Applications Award (1995), IEEE Fellow (1994), AAAS Fellow (1984), APS Fellow (1980), International Plasma Chemistry Society Fellow (1980), Guggenheim Fellow (1972), and UC Berkeley Distinguished Teaching Award (1971).
chunks.question: What are some of Lieberman's notable honors and awards?
chunks.answer: Multiple society fellowships and plasma science awards.
